definition: more than a little but not completely

imageKeyword: kid thinking hard at math puzzle

definition: to go up or get higher

definition: even though

definition: except if; if not

imageKeyword: child eating dinner before going outside

definition: not often; rarely
example: Snow seldom falls in this warm town.

definition: in the end, after a long time or much effort
example: If you keep trying, you will eventually learn to ride a bike.

definition: to stay away from something
example: She walked around the mud to avoid getting her new shoes dirty.

definition: to break open suddenly
example: The balloon burst with a loud pop when it hit the thorn.

definition: to put something under the ground
example: The squirrel likes to bury nuts in the yard for winter.

definition: to shout with happiness to show support

definition: to pick one thing from many
example: You may choose any flavor of ice cream you want.

definition: to say something is yours or is true
example: Both children claim the toy is theirs, so the teacher had to help.

definition: to mix or put things together
example: If you combine blue and yellow paint, you get green.

definition: to give an order that must be followed
example: The dog trainer commanded the puppy to sit and stay.

definition: to think with care about something
example: Please consider all the choices before you pick your answer.

definition: to keep going and not stop what you are doing
example: The rain will continue all day, so bring your umbrella.

definition: to have power over something and make it do what you want
example: Use the remote to control which channel the TV shows.

definition: to place something on top of another thing to hide or protect it
example: Please cover the food, so the flies don't land on it.

definition: to go from one side to the other side of a road, river, or area
example: Look both ways before you cross the street.

definition: to press something so hard it breaks into pieces
example: The giant rock fell and crushed the empty box flat.

definition: to be brave enough to try something scary
example: Nobody dared to open the creaky old door in the basement.

definition: to ask for something in a strong, firm way
example: The hungry baby demanded more food by banging on the table.

definition: to grow or change over time
example: A tiny seed can develop into a tall sunflower in just a few weeks.

definition: to show people or vehicles where they must go

definition: to look at something very closely

definition: to swap one thing for another

definition: to make someone feel very happy and full of energy

definition: to think something will happen

definition: to look around a new place to learn about it

definition: to show how you feel using words or actions

definition: to not be able to do something you tried

definition: to move smoothly like water

definition: to make or shape something into a new thing

definition: to lead someone by going with them and showing the path

definition: to move or do something faster

definition: to put yourself or something where no one can see

definition: to take a long walk outdoors, usually on a trail

definition: to put your arms around someone to show love

definition: to chase or search for animals or things you want to catch

definition: to make something bigger in size or amount

definition: to teach someone by explaining the steps

definition: to look at something and think it is very good

definition: to tell everyone something important

definition: to say yes or agree that something is okay

definition: to disagree with someone using words

definition: to act in a good or right way

definition: to do something special because of a happy event

definition: to look at two things to see how they are the same or different

definition: to get someone to agree with you

definition: to protect someone or something from danger

definition: to go away so no one can see it anymore

definition: to say kind words to help someone keep trying

definition: to get better at something

definition: to start talking when someone else is already speaking

definition: to watch something carefully

definition: to put things in order so they are neat

definition: not having done anything wrong

definition: unable to be seen

definition: very interested and eager to do something

definition: full of energy and fun

definition: clean and not mixed with anything else

definition: not polite, saying or doing unkind things

definition: covered in a rough, brownish layer that forms when metal gets wet

definition: thin and graceful
example: The slender candle fit perfectly in the tiny holder.

definition: not hard, gentle to touch
example: The soft kitten fur felt like a cloud when she petted it.

example: The clown wore an absurd hat that was shaped like a giant banana.

definition: right and without mistakes

example: The brittle twig snapped in half when he stepped on it.

definition: having many bright colors
example: The colorful parrot had red, blue, and yellow feathers.

definition: wanting to hurt others on purpose
example: It is cruel to pull a cat's tail because it causes pain.

definition: brave enough to do dangerous things
example: The daring pilot flew through the storm to deliver medicine.

definition: very dirty, covered with grime or mess
example: His clothes were filthy after playing in the mud all afternoon.

definition: very beautiful in a way that gets your attention
example: The gorgeous sunset turned the sky orange and pink.

definition: very bad or unpleasant
example: The horrible smell from the garbage made everyone hold their nose.

definition: hard to explain or understand
example: A mysterious light appeared in the forest at night.

definition: nice and enjoyable
example: The pleasant breeze cooled us down on the hot summer day.

definition: having great strength
example: The powerful wind blew the leaves off every tree on the street.

definition: so silly it makes you laugh
example: The dog looked ridiculous wearing a tiny hat and sunglasses.

definition: not joking, meaning what you say
example: She had a serious look on her face when she told us the rules.

definition: perfectly clean with no dirt at all
example: After cleaning all day, the kitchen was spotless.

definition: very big or very great
example: The audience gave a tremendous cheer that shook the whole room.

definition: able to be seen
example: The lighthouse was visible from miles away because of its bright light.

definition: not strong, lacking power or energy

definition: having a lot of money
example: The wealthy man built a library, so everyone could read for free.

definition: very bad or evil
example: The wicked witch in the story tried to trick the children.

definition: having no value at all
example: The broken toy was worthless because none of the pieces worked.

definition: in a quick way, using only a few words or moments

definition: again and again, so often it feels like it never ends
example: The puppy constantly wagged its tail because it was always happy.

definition: in one long stretch without any break at all
example: The river flows continuously, never stopping day or night.

definition: happening right now
example: She is currently reading a book about dinosaurs.

definition: in the past, before now
example: The building was formerly a school, but now it is a library.

definition: happening in one second with no delay
example: The light turned on instantly when she flipped the switch.

definition: almost all, but not completely
example: The jar was mostly empty—only a few candies were left.

definition: almost but not quite
example: She nearly finished the race but tripped right before the finish line.

definition: in the regular way, when nothing special changes
example: We normally eat breakfast at seven, but today we slept late.

definition: at first, before things changed
example: The house was originally painted white, but now it is blue.

definition: not all the way, only some
example: The sky was partly cloudy, so we could still see some blue.

definition: maybe, there is a chance it could happen
example: It will possibly snow tonight, so keep your boots by the door.

definition: at this moment; now
example: He is presently working on his art project in the other room.

definition: at an earlier time in the past, not recently
example: He had previously visited the museum, so he knew where to find the dinosaur bones.

definition: very likely to happen
example: It will probably rain because the sky is full of dark clouds.

definition: right on time or right away
example: Class starts at nine, and the teacher arrives promptly every day.

definition: happening at the same time again and again
example: She regularly waters her plant every morning before school.

definition: in a little while, very soon
example: The movie will start shortly, so find your seat now.

definition: easily, nothing more
example: You can fix it simply by pressing the restart button.

definition: now and then, not always
example: Sometimes we walk to school, and sometimes we take the bus.

definition: in a smooth, even way without stopping
example: The rain fell steadily all afternoon without getting worse or letting up.

definition: even now, continuing
example: It is ten o'clock, and she is still reading her book.

definition: in the most common way for that kind of thing
example: He typically walks to school, but today his mom drove him.

definition: more often than not
example: We usually have pizza on Friday nights.

definition: at the exact same moment, with two actions happening together
example: The two friends jumped into the pool simultaneously and made a huge splash.

definition: at the very beginning, before anything changed
example: She was initially scared of the water, but soon she loved swimming.

definition: stays the same and never ends
example: The marker stain permanently stayed on the shirt even after washing.

definition: for a limited time, with plans to go back to normal later
example: The road is temporarily closed while workers fix the broken pipe.

definition: during the past few days or weeks, very recently
example: It has been very cold lately, so we wear extra layers.

definition: during the night while you sleep
example: The snow fell overnight, and everything was white in the morning.

definition: happening one time each year
example: The town has a yearly parade every summer with music and floats.

definition: happening every hour
example: The clock chimes hourly, so you hear it ring twelve times at noon.

definition: a picture in a book that shows what is happening
example: The illustration showed a dragon flying over a mountain.

definition: a drawing that shows how something works
example: The diagram in our science book shows the parts of a flower.

definition: all the words a person knows
example: Reading every day helps you grow your vocabulary.

definition: what a word means
example: Look up the definition in the dictionary if you don't know the word.

definition: something that is true and can be proven
example: It is a fact that the Earth goes around the Sun.

definition: a short telling of the most important parts
example: Write a summary of the story in just three sentences.

definition: what something is mainly about or the subject being talked about
example: The topic of today's lesson is the water cycle.

definition: the end or final part
example: At the conclusion of the movie, the hero saved the day.

definition: a short part of a book or article
example: Read the passage on page five and answer the questions.

definition: looking for facts to learn about something
example: We did research on penguins by reading three different books.

definition: a way of doing something
example: The teacher showed us a new method for solving math problems.

definition: a test to find out what happens
example: Our experiment showed that plants grow faster in sunlight.

definition: any living animal
example: We saw a strange creature with eight legs in the garden—it was a spider!

definition: wetness; water you can feel on a surface or in the air
example: There was moisture on the grass every morning from the cool night air.

definition: what something is made of, like cloth or metal
example: The material of his jacket was soft and kept him very warm.

definition: the place or thing where something first comes from
example: The river's source is a small spring high up in the mountains.

definition: the act of moving or changing position
example: The motion of the swing going back and forth made her laugh.

definition: two or more things stirred together
example: The cake batter is a mixture of flour, eggs, and sugar.

definition: the way something is moving or pointing
example: The wind changed direction and started blowing from the north.

definition: a place that protects you from weather or danger
example: The hikers found shelter under a big rock when it started to rain.

definition: an animal that hunts other animals for food
example: The hawk is a predator that catches mice and small birds.

definition: an animal that others hunt and eat
example: The rabbit is prey for foxes, so it runs very fast to escape.

definition: a gas in the air that we breathe to stay alive
example: Trees make oxygen, which is why forests help us breathe clean air.

definition: a huge group of stars in space
example: Our sun is just one star in a galaxy with billions of others.

definition: the remains of a plant or animal from long ago, kept safe inside rock
example: We found a fossil of a leaf that was millions of years old.

definition: a solid natural thing found in the ground
example: Gold is a mineral that people dig out of the earth.

definition: a steady flow of water or air moving in one direction
example: The strong current in the river pushed the boat downstream.

definition: tiny drops of water floating in the air like a gas
example: Steam from the hot soup rose into the air as water vapor.

definition: the slow wearing away of rock or soil by wind or water
example: Erosion from the river carved a deep valley over thousands of years.

definition: the path something takes as it moves around another thing in space
example: The Earth takes one year to complete its orbit around the Sun.

definition: the force that pulls things down toward the ground
example: Gravity is what makes a ball fall back down after you throw it up.

definition: a group of living things that are the same kind
example: There are many species of birds, from tiny hummingbirds to huge eagles.

definition: a group of people who live in the same area
example: Our community worked together to build a new playground.

definition: all the people living in one place
example: The population of our town grew when new families moved in.

definition: the way of life of a group of people
example: In their culture, families gather every Sunday for a big meal.

definition: a family member who lived long before you
example: Her ancestors came from a small village across the ocean.

definition: all the people born around the same time
example: Kids, parents, and grandparents are three different generations.

definition: a person who helps without being paid
example: Volunteers helped clean up the beach after the storm.

definition: a person who buys and sells things
example: The merchant at the market sold fresh fruits and vegetables.

definition: one of the first people to go to a new place or try something new
example: The pioneers traveled west in covered wagons to find new land.

definition: the leader of a country or group
example: The president of the club welcomed all the new members.

definition: a job that needs special training
example: Being a doctor is a profession that requires many years of school.

definition: a friend who goes with you
example: The dog was her loyal companion on every walk through the woods.

definition: a person who takes care of someone
example: His guardian made sure he ate healthy food and went to bed on time.

definition: a person who moves to a new country to live
example: Many immigrants came to this country to find a better life.

definition: a person whose job is to check that things are done right
example: The inspector looked at the building to make sure it was safe.

definition: a person who carries a message from one place to another
example: The messenger rode his horse fast to deliver the important letter.

definition: a person in your family
example: Every relative came to the big reunion at the park.

definition: a person who studies and learns a lot
example: The scholar read hundreds of books about ancient history.

definition: a person who sees something happen
example: She was a witness to the accident and told the police what she saw.

definition: something that gives you a better chance to succeed
example: Being tall is an advantage in basketball because you can reach the hoop.

definition: being brave when you are scared
example: It took courage to stand up and speak in front of the whole school.

definition: trying hard to do something
example: She put a lot of effort into her science project and won first place.

definition: things you know from learning
example: Reading books gives you knowledge about the world.

definition: being able to wait without getting upset
example: It takes patience to grow a garden because plants need weeks to get big.

definition: a job or duty that you must do
example: Feeding the class pet is my responsibility this week.

definition: a great win or success
example: Our team's triumph in the championship game made the whole school cheer.

definition: something bad that happens without being planned
example: She spilled the milk by accident when her elbow bumped the glass.

definition: the way you think and feel about something
example: A positive attitude helps you keep trying even when things are hard.

definition: a line that shows where one area ends and another begins
example: The fence is the boundary between our yard and the neighbor's yard.

definition: a result caused by something you did
example: The consequence of forgetting her lunch was being hungry all afternoon.

definition: a talk between two or more people
example: They had a long conversation about their favorite books.

definition: a choice you make after thinking
example: Choosing which school to attend was a big decision for the family.

definition: proof that shows something is true
example: Muddy footprints were evidence that someone walked through the garden.

definition: something you live through and remember
example: Visiting the ocean for the first time was an amazing experience.

definition: the part of your mind that makes up new ideas and pictures
example: He used his imagination to pretend the cardboard box was a spaceship.

definition: a special time or event
example: A birthday is a happy occasion for cake and presents.

definition: the answer to a problem
example: Working together was the solution to finishing the project on time.

definition: to examine something closely to check if it is correct or safe
example: The teacher inspected our projects to make sure they were finished.

definition: to choose something carefully
example: Please select one book from the shelf to read this week.

definition: to give or provide something that is needed
example: The school asked the store to supply paper and pencils for every classroom.

definition: to stay alive through something risky
example: The little plant survived the cold winter and bloomed in spring.

definition: to change completely into something different
example: The caterpillar will transform into a beautiful butterfly.

definition: to carry something from one place to another
example: Trucks transport food from farms to grocery stores.

definition: to figure out what something is
example: Can you identify which bird is singing by listening to its call?

definition: to get something from a family member
example: She inherited her grandmother's blue eyes and curly hair.

definition: very large and heavy
example: The massive boulder was too big for anyone to move.

definition: much smaller than usual
example: She built a miniature house for her dolls out of cardboard.

definition: very easy to see or understand
example: It was obvious the dog ate the cake because crumbs were all over its face.

definition: not special; just like the rest
example: It seemed like an ordinary day until the surprise party started.

definition: one specific thing out of many, chosen for a special reason
example: She wanted that particular book, not any of the other ones.

definition: strange or odd in a way that catches your eye
example: The peculiar noise coming from the closet sounded like a squeaky toy.

definition: happening very quickly
example: The rapid river moved so fast that logs were swept away in seconds.

definition: not wanting to do something, slow to act because of doubt
example: He was reluctant to jump in the pool because the water looked cold.

definition: easily hurt or bothered
example: His sensitive skin turned red when he stayed in the sun too long.

definition: very serious or very bad
example: The severe storm brought strong winds that knocked down power lines.

definition: right for a certain purpose
example: Sneakers are suitable shoes for running but not for a fancy dinner.

definition: the right amount needed, not too little and not too much
example: Make sure you have sufficient water for the whole hike.

definition: empty and not being used, with no people or things inside
example: The vacant house had no furniture and no people living in it.

definition: extremely large and wide
example: The vast ocean stretched as far as the eye could see.

definition: very bright and easy to picture in your mind
example: She painted with vivid colors that made the flowers look real.

definition: to have or own something as your own
example: The old pirate was said to possess a chest full of gold coins.

definition: to show something that was hidden
example: She pulled back the curtain to reveal the surprise behind it.

definition: to give someone a job or task to do
example: The teacher will assign each student a different topic to research.

definition: to answer or reply
example: When the teacher asks a question, raise your hand to respond.

definition: to need something before you can move forward
example: This recipe requires three eggs and a cup of milk.

definition: to be against something or disagree with it
example: Some students opposed the new rule because they thought it was unfair.

definition: to fill or use a space by being in it
example: Boxes and old chairs occupy most of the space in the garage.

definition: to do something in front of people
example: The students will perform a play for their parents on Friday.

definition: to finish something well
example: She accomplished her goal of reading fifty books in one year.

definition: very careful so nothing bad happens
example: The cautious girl looked both ways twice before crossing the street.

definition: able to do something well
example: He is capable of running a mile without stopping.

definition: something you really need
example: Water is essential for all living things to survive.

definition: lucky, having good things happen
example: We were fortunate that the rain stopped just before our picnic.

definition: so amazing it is hard to believe
example: The magician did an incredible trick that made a dove appear from a hat.

definition: very many, a large number of
example: There were numerous stars in the sky that night, too many to count.

definition: sure something is true, or feeling hopeful
example: She was positive she left her keys on the table because she saw them there.

definition: coming before in time or order
example: In the previous chapter, the hero found a magic map.

definition: a large area of land
example: The desert region gets very little rain all year.

definition: something that has been built, like a building or bridge
example: The bridge is a strong structure made of steel and stone.

definition: a picture or mark that stands for something else
example: A heart is a symbol that means love.

definition: something families or groups do the same way year after year
example: We bake cookies every December—it is a family tradition.

definition: a party or special event to show happiness
example: The town held a celebration with fireworks when the team won.

definition: something that is hard to do and tests your skill
example: Climbing the mountain was a challenge, but they made it to the top.

definition: tools or things you need to do a job
example: The firefighter put on all her equipment before going into the building.

definition: a long trip, usually by sea
example: The sailors went on a voyage across the ocean that lasted three months.

definition: an area of land that belongs to someone
example: The wolf marked its territory so other animals would stay away.

definition: a very bad event that causes great harm
example: The flood was a disaster that damaged many homes in the town.

definition: only, nothing more than
example: She was merely trying to help, nothing more.

definition: in an exact way, with no mistakes
example: The code must follow the rules precisely, or the computer won't understand it.

definition: completely, all the way
example: The glass was entirely full, with no room for even one more drop.

definition: almost not, hardly
example: He could scarcely believe he won the spelling bee.

definition: moving very quickly
example: The deer swiftly ran through the forest and disappeared.

definition: happy to do something without being forced
example: He willingly shared his lunch with the new student.

definition: to keep something in good shape
example: You need to maintain your bike by keeping the tires full of air.

definition: to start or create something new
example: The students established a recycling club at their school.

definition: to work together with others
example: The whole class had to cooperate to finish the mural on time.

definition: to stand for or speak for something
example: The star on the flag represents the state we live in.

definition: to think very hard about one thing
example: It is hard to concentrate on homework when the TV is on.

definition: to make things in a factory, usually in large amounts
example: The factory manufactures toys that are sold all over the world.

definition: to show how something works
example: The teacher demonstrated how to mix paint colors on the paper.

definition: to look into something to find the truth
example: The detective investigated the missing cookies from the jar.

definition: something new that you find or learn about for the first time
example: We made a great discovery—a bird nest in our tree!

definition: a punishment for breaking a rule
example: The penalty for being late to class was missing five minutes of free time.

definition: how much or how many of something there is
example: She bought a large quantity of apples to make pies for the whole family.

definition: fair and making good sense
example: The price of the book was reasonable, so she had enough money to buy it.

definition: to come closer to something
example: The cat quietly approached the bird, trying not to scare it.

definition: to fall down suddenly
example: The tower of blocks collapsed when the baby bumped the table.

definition: raised by people and living in homes, not wild
example: Dogs and cats are domestic animals that live with people.

definition: on the outside of something, not inside
example: The external walls of the house were painted bright yellow.

definition: on the inside of something, not outside
example: The doctor checked her internal organs to make sure she was healthy.

definition: once a year
example: The school's annual science fair is always in March.

definition: being allowed to do something by someone in charge
example: You need permission from your parents before you go on the field trip.

definition: to know what something is because you have seen it before
example: I didn't recognize him at first because he was wearing a costume.

definition: the power to change how someone thinks or acts
example: Her older sister had a big influence on what music she liked.

definition: to explain what something means
example: She interpreted the French menu for her friends.

definition: to change so you fit a new situation
example: Animals adapt to new places by changing how they eat and sleep.

definition: a good result or helpful thing you get
example: One benefit of wearing a helmet is that it protects your head.

definition: strong and able to last a long time
example: Her durable lunchbox did not crack even when it fell on the floor.

definition: real and not fake
example: His apology was genuine, so his friend forgave him.

definition: to pause because you are not sure
example: She hesitated at the diving board, then took a deep breath and jumped.

definition: to talk to agree on a fair plan
example: The sisters negotiated and chose to split the last cookie in half.

definition: not lasting forever, only for now
example: The sign was temporary, so workers took it down after the road was fixed.

definition: you can count on it to work or be true
example: Our reliable bus driver came on time each day.

definition: to soak up a liquid
example: The towel can absorb the water from the spilled juice.

definition: to pull something toward you
example: Bright flowers attract bees because the bees want nectar.

definition: to guess what will happen next
example: Can you predict what will happen next in the story?

definition: to ask politely for something
example: She requested a book about sharks from the library.

definition: to make less; to lower the amount
example: To reduce noise, the class spoke in whispers during the test.

definition: to put parts together
example: We assembled the puzzle pieces. The whole picture appeared.

definition: to gather more and more over time
example: If you save a little each week, your money will accumulate quickly.

definition: to show that you noticed or agree
example: She nodded to acknowledge his question.

definition: just barely enough to get the job done
example: We had adequate food for the whole trip.

definition: to speak up for something you believe in
example: He advocates for recycling at his school.

definition: to set aside for a purpose
example: She decided to allocate half her allowance to savings and half to spending.

definition: not clear; could mean more than one thing
example: The directions were ambiguous, so we got lost.

definition: to change something to fix or improve it
example: He amended his essay after the teacher's comments.

definition: close to the real number but not exact
example: The approximate distance is ten miles.

definition: to dream of doing something great
example: She aspires to become a doctor one day.

definition: to say something strongly and clearly
example: He asserted that his answer was correct.

definition: to give permission for something
example: The principal authorized the field trip.

definition: helpful or good for you
example: Eating fruit is beneficial for your health.

definition: leaning toward one side unfairly
example: A good judge should not show bias.

definition: the most that something can hold or carry
example: The jar has a capacity of fifty marbles.

definition: to explain something so it is easier to understand
example: Can you clarify what you mean? I do not understand the question.

definition: to fall on the same date or time as another event, usually by chance
example: My birthday will coincide with the school carnival this year.

definition: spoken or written opinions about an event
example: The sports commentary made the game more exciting.

definition: to make up for something or pay someone back
example: The store compensated her with a new toy after the broken one.

definition: to put together facts from many places
example: She compiled a list of her favorite songs.

definition: something that goes well with another thing
example: The red scarf was a nice complement to his coat.

definition: to do what is asked or required
example: Students must comply with the school rules.

definition: to think of an idea
example: She conceived a clever plan to surprise her friend.

definition: to keep within limits
example: The dog was confined to the backyard.

definition: permission or agreement that something is okay to do
example: You need your parents' consent to go on the trip.

definition: happening as a result of something
example: The heavy rain and the consequent flooding closed the road.

definition: to bring things together into one
example: She consolidated her notes into one notebook.

definition: a limit or rule that holds you back
example: Time was a constraint, so we had to work fast.

definition: to ask an expert for advice
example: He consulted the librarian to find the right book.

definition: to think about something deeply
example: She sat and contemplated what to write in her story.

definition: to say the opposite of what someone else said
example: His actions contradicted his words.

definition: a big argument among people about an important topic
example: There was controversy over where to build the new park.

definition: to come together for a meeting
example: The club members convened in the library.

definition: to match or be similar to
example: The answers correspond to the questions on the test.

definition: rules used to judge or decide something
example: The criteria for the art contest were neatness and color.

definition: the type of money a country uses
example: The dollar is the currency of the United States.

definition: not having enough of something needed
example: A deficiency of sunlight can make plants turn yellow.

definition: to be a sign of or to mean
example: A red light denotes that you should stop.

definition: to get something from a source
example: Many English words derive from Latin.

definition: to go a different way from what is usual
example: He deviated from the trail and got lost.
